title: units: tidy up dynbuf init
body:
Init dynbuf after global init to bring closer to use, improve
readability and sync test sources.
author: vszakats

issue_comments.body: How do moving these change anything? The `curlx_dyn_init` call does not allocate anything.
Is it a tidy-up even? I don't see the point...

To me it's confusing that it's at different places in different tests,
and that it's done before the global init. Why not init closer to where
the thing is used?

IMO this is like the higher later debate, neither one is wrong and both are acceptable, though historically we have tended to just go with whatever is already there unless there is a strong reason to change.
